mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through improved profit expectations and reduced recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 350106M in Aug 2005 reflects robust consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS momentum fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5% and remains elevated for 2+ months, monetary tightening constrains liquidity and equity valuations compress, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the economy slows.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.5% in Aug 2005 marks tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 3.5% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5.0% signals tight labor markets and wage pressure, which increases corporate cost structures and reduces profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% in Aug 2005 indicates tight labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with wage inflation risk
failure_condition: Negative UNRATE momentum fails to predict negative 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates an overheating economy approaching capacity constraints. This interaction triggers expectations of Fed tightening and inflation, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 119.678 and UNRATE at 4.9% in Aug 2005 suggest tight capacity
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 5% month-over-month, signaling weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction. This predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as construction and related sectors deteriorate.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2042K in Jul 2005 provides baseline for decline detection
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown or housing cycle downturn
failure_condition: HOUST declines > 5% fail to predict negative 60d returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 200K per month while UNRATE remains below 5.0%, signaling a tight labor market that forces wage acceleration and inflation expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as inflation risk reprices.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133999K and UNRATE at 4.9% in Aug 2005
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage inflation risk
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative 5d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 4.0%, indicating monetary policy is accommodative relative to inflation. This mismatch triggers expectations of future Fed tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 196.1 and FEDFUNDS at 3.5% in Aug 2005
regime_hint: monetary policy behind the curve with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Policy-lag signal fails to predict negative 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 3.0% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) remain elevated above 2000K units, indicating broad-based demand expansion. This sustained growth signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through earnings growth and multiple expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11088.6B and HOUST at 2042K in Q2 2005
regime_hint: broad-based economic expansion with strong demand
failure_condition: Dual-expansion signal fails to predict positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
